title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat but could be exploited if malicious actors modify or distribute a compromised version. Additionally, over-reliance on automated tools without proper validation could lead to missed vulnerabilities or false positives.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and ensure it is obtained from a trusted repository before use.
Use the tool as a supplementary measure alongside other security practices, not as a sole solution.
Monitor for updates or advisories related to the tool to ensure its continued security and effectiveness.

description: A vulnerability was determined in omec-project amf up to 2.1.3-dev. Impacted is the function NGSetupRequest of the file ngap/handler.go. Executing a manipulation of the argument InformationElement can lead to memory corruption. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. Upgrading to version 2.2.0 is recommended to address this issue. The affected component should be upgraded. The same pull request fixes multiple security issues.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in omec-project amf up to version 2.1.3-dev could lead to memory corruption through manipulation of the InformationElement argument in the NGSetupRequest function. This could potentially allow remote attackers to exploit the system, though the severity is categorized as low. The public disclosure of the exploit increases the risk of malicious use.
recommended_actions: Upgrade the omec-project amf component to version 2.2.0 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Conduct a thorough review of system logs to identify any potential exploitation attempts.
Implement additional monitoring and intrusion detection mechanisms to detect unusual activity targeting the NGSetupRequest function.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in omec-project amf up to 2.1.3-dev. The impacted element is...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in omec-project amf up to 2.1.3-dev. The impacted element is the function RANConfiguration of the file ngap/handler.go. The manipulation results in null pointer dereference. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. Upgrading to version 2.2.0 is sufficient to resolve this issue. Upgrading the affected component is recommended. The same pull request fixes multiple security issues.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The discovered security flaw in omec-project amf up to 2.1.3-dev could lead to a null pointer dereference, potentially causing application crashes or denial of service. The vulnerability can be exploited remotely, and a public exploit is available, increasing the likelihood of attacks. However, the severity is considered low as the impact is limited to service disruption without data compromise or privilege escalation.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade omec-project amf to version 2.2.0 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Review and monitor systems for any signs of exploitation, especially if running affected versions.
Implement network-level protections, such as firewalls or intrusion detection systems, to detect and block potential exploitation attempts.

title: A vulnerability was found in vercel ai up to 3.0.97. The affected element is the function...
description: A vulnerability was found in vercel ai up to 3.0.97. The affected element is the function validateDownloadUrl of the file packages/provider-utils/src/download-blob.ts of the component provider-utils. The manipulation results in server-side request forgery. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the validateDownloadUrl function of vercel ai up to version 3.0.97 allows for server-side request forgery (SSRF), which could enable attackers to manipulate server requests, access internal systems, or exfiltrate sensitive data. The exploit is public, increasing the likelihood of malicious use.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the vercel ai package to a patched version if available, or apply temporary mitigations such as input validation and request filtering.
Implement strict network segmentation and firewall rules to limit the impact of SSRF attacks on internal systems.
Monitor server logs for unusual or unauthorized external requests and deploy intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title:
Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions through 1.94 for Perl truncates passwords with embedded NULLs.
...
description:
Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions through 1.94 for Perl truncates passwords with embedded NULLs.

Password parameters in PKCS12.xs are declared char *, which routes through Perl's default typemap to SvPV_nolen.  The Perl length is discarded.

The C code (or OpenSSL internally) calls strlen() on the buffer.  Any password byte at or after the first NULL is silently dropped. Binary / KDF-derived / HMAC-derived passwords lose entropy without any warnings.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8721.

title:
Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions through 1.94 for Perl have out of bound (OOB) write flaws.

When...
description:
Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions through 1.94 for Perl have out of bound (OOB) write flaws.

When parsing a PKCS12 file, with a >= 1 GiB OCTET STRING (or BIT STRING) attribute on a SAFEBAG, via info() or info_as_hash(), a heap-OOB-WRITE would be triggered which could have Remote Code Execution (RCE) potential.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8507.

title: CVE-2026-8266 - A vulnerability was detected in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This affects the function gsm_build_pdu_session
description: A vulnerability was detected in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This affects the function gsm_build_pdu_session_establishment_accept of the file /src/smf/gsm-build.c of the component SMF. The manipulation results in denial of service. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Open5GS allows remote attackers to exploit the gsm_build_pdu_session_establishment_accept function, potentially causing a denial of service (DoS) in the SMF component. This could disrupt mobile network operations, including 4G/5G core networks, leading to service outages and degraded performance.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply network-level mitigations, such as rate limiting and traffic filtering, to reduce the risk of exploitation.
Monitor Open5GS project updates and apply any patches or updates as soon as they are released.
Conduct a thorough review of your Open5GS deployment and implement additional security measures, such as isolating the SMF component and enabling logging to detect potential exploitation attempts.
